description: Not a Line in Sagastas Reply That Could Offend This Government 1 V 5 Claimed the President His No Kight to Htop Filibustering New York Nov 11The Journals Washington special says It is con tended here by prominent lawyers that the Spanish government has an entirely erroneous idea about the power of the executive department to prevent filibus tering and that the executive depart ment is now violating the law in at tempting to be agreeable to the Spanish government in the matter of stopping vessels loaded with arms bound for Cuba It is also contended that both the present and the Cleveland administra tions have willfully misled the general public as to the law reating to the tak ing of munitions of war to Cuba The law as laid down by these lawyers is Spain has a treaty wilh the United States by which each country agrees not to assist an enemy of the other The Spanish government through its representative has fficially informed the United States government there is no war in Cuba that the Cubans are simply violating the municipal law of Spain and the Spanish government is endeavoring to enforce that law and punish violators that
